```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  m <- sample(2:6,1); momento <- sample(seq(1,20,0.5),1)
  coef <- momento/factorial(m)
  vals <- round(c(momento,coef,factorial(m)*momento,momento^m,momento/m),4)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.4f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Na série de Maclaurin de uma fgm, o coeficiente de $t^{`r m`}$ é `r sprintf("%.4f",coef)`. Qual é $E(X^{`r m`})$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A expansão é
$$M(t)=\sum_{j=0}^{\infty}\frac{E(X^j)}{j!}t^j.$$
Logo, $E(X^{`r m`})=`r m`!\,(`r sprintf("%.4f",coef)`)
=`r sprintf("%.4f",momento)`$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S9Q9_Maclaurin_momentos
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
